diveLevel: Advanced + 50 dives for the Bianca C; Open Water for sculpture park.

quotes.text: Swimming over the Bianca C's bow at 30m as schools of jacks pour past — one of the best wrecks I've dived anywhere.

goodToKnow.title: Getting there
goodToKnow.body: Fly Grenada (GND) direct from many US/UK hubs.

goodToKnow.title: Sculpture park
goodToKnow.body: Worth a separate dive — Jason deCaires Taylor's installation.

extendedDescription: The Pit is a cenote-style sinkhole in Belize's inland jungle — less famous than Mexico's Yucatán cenotes but offering similar geology with virtually no crowds. Light beams, halocline, deep dark depths.
seasonNotes: Year-round; rainy season (June–November) can affect viz.
tripDuration: Day-trip or 2–3 night cave-focused stay.
diveStyle: Cavern/cave diving; no current; depths 5–40m.
diveLevel: Open Water for cavern lines; Cave certification for further.

quotes.text: Mexico's cenotes get all the press but Belize has its own. Often you'll have it to yourself.

goodToKnow.title: Getting there
goodToKnow.body: Belize City + inland drive. Combine with reef diving for variety.

goodToKnow.title: Bring lights
goodToKnow.body: Strong primary plus backup is mandatory; ops can rent.

extendedDescription: The Poor Knights Islands off New Zealand's east coast are a marine reserve Jacques Cousteau called one of the world's top ten dive sites — volcanic arches, caves, big schools, and subtropical species at higher latitude than they should be.
seasonNotes: Year-round; January–May has warmest water (19–24°C) and best viz. Winter dips to 14°C.
tripDuration: Day-trips from Tutukaka or 2–3 night stays.
diveStyle: Boat diving on volcanic walls and caves; mild-to-moderate current.
diveLevel: Open Water; Advanced for some swim-throughs and depth.

quotes.text: Subtropical fish that shouldn't be at this latitude, in water that shouldn't be this clear. Poor Knights breaks the rules.

goodToKnow.title: Getting there
goodToKnow.body: Fly Auckland, 2.5h drive north to Tutukaka.

goodToKnow.title: Cold water
goodToKnow.body: 7mm year-round; many divers use semi-dry or drysuit in winter.

extendedDescription: Milford Sound on New Zealand's South Island is a fjord with a freshwater tannin-stained surface layer that mimics deep-water conditions just below the surface — black coral, basket stars, and species normally found at 100m+ accessible at recreational depth.
seasonNotes: Year-round; winter (June–August) has clearest conditions but coldest air.
tripDuration: 2–3 nights as part of a South Island trip.
diveStyle: Drysuit diving on fjord walls; no current.
diveLevel: Drysuit cert required; Advanced helps.

quotes.text: Deep-water animals at 15m because the fresh water blocks their light — unlike anywhere else in diving.

goodToKnow.title: Getting there
goodToKnow.body: Fly Queenstown, 4h drive to Milford. Or charter flight.

goodToKnow.title: Water temp
goodToKnow.body: 10–14°C; drysuit only.

extendedDescription: The Cod Hole is the most famous dive site on the northern Great Barrier Reef — a sand bowl populated by huge potato cod (giant grouper) that swim up to divers. Combined with Osprey Reef and the Ribbon Reefs on a typical liveaboard.
seasonNotes: October–December is peak with warm water and minke whales (June–July). Year-round divable.
tripDuration: 4–7 night liveaboard from Cairns or Port Douglas.
diveStyle: Reef and pinnacle diving with moderate current; sand-bowl interaction at the Cod Hole.
diveLevel: Open Water for Cod Hole; Advanced for Osprey Reef and deeper sites.

quotes.text: A 60kg potato cod gently bumping your fin is the most personable interaction I've ever had on a reef.

goodToKnow.title: Getting there
goodToKnow.body: Fly Cairns (CNS), boat from there.

goodToKnow.title: Reef tax
goodToKnow.body: Australian reef tax + park fees added by operator.

extendedDescription: Ningaloo Reef on Western Australia's coast is the world's most accessible whale-shark swim — fly out from Exmouth, find a whale shark, slip in next to it. Plus humpback whales (in season), mantas, and pristine fringing reef.
seasonNotes: Whale sharks March–August (peak April–June). Humpbacks July–November. Mantas year-round, peak May–November.
tripDuration: 5–7 nights from Exmouth.
diveStyle: Snorkel for whale sharks; scuba on fringing reef and outer reef.
diveLevel: Snorkel for whale shark trips; Open Water for scuba.

quotes.text: Walking onto a beach, looking left and seeing whale sharks two minutes offshore — Ningaloo makes it feel ordinary.

goodToKnow.title: Getting there
goodToKnow.body: Fly Perth → Learmonth (LEA), 30 min to Exmouth.

goodToKnow.title: Whale shark code
goodToKnow.body: Strict no-touch, distance rules. Operators enforce.

extendedDescription: Julian Rocks off Byron Bay is one of Australia's best shore-accessible dive sites — a marine reserve where tropical and temperate species mix on a small offshore pinnacle, with grey nurse sharks in season, leopard sharks, and turtles.
seasonNotes: Grey nurse sharks June–November. Leopard sharks December–May. Year-round divable.
tripDuration: 2–3 nights as part of an Australia trip.
diveStyle: Boat diving (15-min ride) on a small pinnacle; mild current.
diveLevel: Open Water.

quotes.text: Grey nurse sharks at the Cod Hole at Julian Rocks — accessible big-shark diving without leaving NSW.

goodToKnow.title: Getting there
goodToKnow.body: Fly Gold Coast (OOL) or Ballina (BNK), drive to Byron Bay.

goodToKnow.title: Marine park
goodToKnow.body: Strict protections; no fishing, no touching.

extendedDescription: Kimbe Bay on PNG's New Britain island is one of the planet's biodiversity hotspots — over 850 reef fish species recorded, healthy coral, and almost no other divers. Walindi Plantation Resort and a couple of liveaboards serve the bay.
seasonNotes: April–December is best. January–March has occasional rain and reduced viz.
tripDuration: 7-night land-based or liveaboard.
diveStyle: Reef and pinnacle diving with mild-to-moderate current.
diveLevel: Open Water; Advanced for deeper pinnacles.

quotes.text: Coral biodiversity that makes Indonesia look thin. PNG is the secret nobody talks about.

goodToKnow.title: Getting there
goodToKnow.body: Fly Port Moresby → Hoskins (HKN), 1h transfer.

goodToKnow.title: Malaria zone
goodToKnow.body: Prophylaxis essential.

extendedDescription: Milne Bay at PNG's eastern tip is muck diving paradise — black-sand bottoms hosting rare critters, plus the wreck of the Pacific Gas (a Japanese WWII tanker) and healthy reefs further out. Featured in Bob Halstead's early muck-diving books.
seasonNotes: April–November is best. Closed by monsoon December–March in some operations.
tripDuration: 7-night liveaboard from Alotau.
diveStyle: Slow muck diving and reef drift; mild current.
diveLevel: Open Water.

quotes.text: Bob Halstead literally invented muck diving here. The critters are still showing up.

goodToKnow.title: Getting there
goodToKnow.body: Fly Port Moresby → Gurney (GUR) at Alotau.

goodToKnow.title: Malaria
goodToKnow.body: Prophylaxis essential.

extendedDescription: Beqa Lagoon off Fiji's main island is the country's most famous shark dive — bull sharks, tiger sharks (in season), and a half-dozen other species at a managed baited site. Pacific Harbour is the staging town.
seasonNotes: Year-round; April–October has best viz. Tigers November–April.
tripDuration: 3–5 nights from Pacific Harbour.
diveStyle: Baited shark dive at 18–25m; mild current. Plus surrounding soft-coral reefs.
diveLevel: Advanced for the shark dive.

quotes.text: Eight bull sharks in formation at 20m — Beqa has the count and the chumming protocol nailed.

goodToKnow.title: Getting there
goodToKnow.body: Fly Nadi (NAN), 3h drive to Pacific Harbour.

goodToKnow.title: Conservation fee
goodToKnow.body: Levy supports local village conservation.

extendedDescription: The Great White Wall in Fiji's Somosomo Strait (Taveuni) is one of the world's premier soft-coral dives — a vertical wall blanketed in white soft coral that blooms in current, with sharks and big pelagics overhead. Often called the soft coral capital of the world.
seasonNotes: April–October is dry season and best viz. Year-round divable.
tripDuration: 5–7 nights based on Taveuni or nearby Vanua Levu.
diveStyle: Wall and drift diving with moderate-to-strong current.
diveLevel: Advanced recommended.

quotes.text: A wall of white soft coral pulsing in current is one of the prettiest sights underwater. Fiji owns it.

goodToKnow.title: Getting there
goodToKnow.body: Fly Nadi → Taveuni (TVU) or Savusavu (SVU).

goodToKnow.title: Current timing
goodToKnow.body: Soft coral blooms only with current. Dive guides time the slack carefully.

extendedDescription: The Rock Islands in Palau's southern lagoon include the famous Jellyfish Lake — a saltwater pond cut off from the sea where golden jellyfish have evolved with reduced stings, allowing snorkelers to swim among millions of them. Plus countless dive sites among the limestone islands.
seasonNotes: Year-round; November–May is drier. Jellyfish population fluctuates with ENSO cycles.
tripDuration: 5–7 nights as part of a Palau trip.
diveStyle: Snorkel-only for Jellyfish Lake; scuba on surrounding island walls and channels.
diveLevel: All levels for jellyfish snorkel; Advanced for Rock Island dives.

quotes.text: Floating in a million pulsing golden jellies in a lake on top of an island — Palau's strangest hour underwater.

goodToKnow.title: Getting there
goodToKnow.body: Boat from Koror.

goodToKnow.title: Permit
goodToKnow.body: Rock Islands permit ($100, 10-day) required.

extendedDescription: Manta Ridge in Yap (Micronesia) is the world's most reliable manta cleaning station — divers kneel at the bottom of a channel and watch mantas queue up. The rest of Yap's diving is reef and channel work, traditional and uncrowded.
seasonNotes: Year-round; December–April is peak manta season (mating). November–May has best viz.
tripDuration: 5–7 nights at one of a few resorts.
diveStyle: Channel diving with moderate current; bottom-kneeling at cleaning stations.
diveLevel: Open Water for cleaning stations; Advanced for channel drifts.

quotes.text: Sitting still on the sand while a manta hovers a meter above you for two minutes — Yap's signature moment.

goodToKnow.title: Getting there
goodToKnow.body: Fly United island-hopper via Guam to YAP.

goodToKnow.title: Cultural respect
goodToKnow.body: Yapese village protocols; ask before photographing people on land.

extendedDescription: The SS President Coolidge is a 200m former luxury liner-turned-troopship that hit mines off Espiritu Santo in 1942 — now one of the world's largest accessible wrecks, with the bow at 20m and stern at 70m+. Multiple dives required to see it.
seasonNotes: Year-round; May–October is driest. Water 28–29°C consistently.
tripDuration: 5–10 nights on Espiritu Santo.
diveStyle: Shore-entry wreck diving (yes, you walk in); penetration on most dives; depths 20–60m.
diveLevel: Advanced + wreck specialty required; tech training for deeper sections.

quotes.text: I've dived the Coolidge thirty times and still find new corridors. It's a wreck-diver's career destination.

goodToKnow.title: Getting there
goodToKnow.body: Fly Port Vila → Santo (SON).

goodToKnow.title: Bring nitrox
goodToKnow.body: Many dives sit at 30–40m for long stretches.

extendedDescription: Rangiroa is the world's second-largest atoll, with two passes (Tiputa, Avatoru) that funnel current and pelagics — grey reef sharks, dolphins (often), hammerheads (in season), tuna, manta rays. The Tuamotu archipelago's gateway atoll.
seasonNotes: Year-round divable; May–November is drier. Hammerheads peak December–March.
tripDuration: 5–7 nights, often combined with Fakarava.
diveStyle: Pass diving on incoming current with negative entries; safety stops in blue water.
diveLevel: Advanced + current comfort.

quotes.text: Dolphins joined our pass dive at Tiputa and surfed the current with us. Polynesia's signature moment.

goodToKnow.title: Getting there
goodToKnow.body: Air Tahiti RGI from Papeete.

goodToKnow.title: Combine with Fakarava
goodToKnow.body: Most divers do both Tuamotu atolls in one trip.

extendedDescription: The Bonegi wrecks (Hirokawa Maru and Kinugawa Maru) on Guadalcanal are shore-divable WWII Japanese transports beached during the Battle of Guadalcanal. Combined with broader Solomons diving, including reef and tech sites.
seasonNotes: April–November is dry season. Year-round divable.
tripDuration: 5–7 nights on Guadalcanal or liveaboard.
diveStyle: Shore-entry wreck diving; penetration on Bonegi 1.
diveLevel: Open Water for exterior; Advanced + wreck for penetration.

quotes.text: Walking into the ocean and onto a Japanese transport from the Solomon campaign — history at hand.

goodToKnow.title: Getting there
goodToKnow.body: Fly Honiara (HIR) via Brisbane or Port Moresby.

goodToKnow.title: Safety advisory
goodToKnow.body: Check current advisories before traveling to Guadalcanal.

extendedDescription: Niue is a tiny raised coral atoll in the South Pacific — the diving is on submarine caves and lava tubes carved into the limestone, with seasonal humpback whales (you can sometimes hear them underwater) and exceptional water clarity.
seasonNotes: Humpbacks July–October. Year-round divable; April–November is driest.
tripDuration: 5–7 nights at one of a few small lodges.
diveStyle: Cave and cavern diving in limestone; mild current.
diveLevel: Advanced for cave routes; Open Water for outer reefs.

quotes.text: We were 18m down in a lava tube and heard humpback song through the rock. Nowhere else.

goodToKnow.title: Getting there
goodToKnow.body: One weekly flight from Auckland.

goodToKnow.title: Whale swim
goodToKnow.body: Niue has a special permit allowing in-water humpback swims — book ahead.

extendedDescription: The Medes Islands off Catalonia's Costa Brava are one of the Mediterranean's longest-protected marine reserves — schools of barracuda and grouper, the SS Reggio Messina wreck nearby, and the densest fish biomass in the Med.
seasonNotes: May–October; July–August is warmest (23°C). Winter dips to 13–15°C.
tripDuration: 3–5 nights from L'Estartit.
diveStyle: Boat diving on rocky walls and pinnacles; mild current.
diveLevel: Open Water; Advanced for the deeper sites.

quotes.text: Forty years of protection and you can taste it underwater. The grouper come up to look at you.

goodToKnow.title: Getting there
goodToKnow.body: Fly Barcelona (BCN), 1.5h drive to L'Estartit.

goodToKnow.title: Park permits
goodToKnow.body: Daily diver quotas; book ahead in summer.

extendedDescription: La Restinga on El Hierro (Canary Islands) is volcanic Atlantic diving — black-sand bottoms, manta rays, devil rays, occasional whale sharks, and the 2011 underwater volcanic eruption site that created new geology.
seasonNotes: Year-round; September–November is calmest. Water 19–24°C.
tripDuration: 5–7 nights at one of several small lodges.
diveStyle: Shore and boat diving on volcanic walls and lava flows; mild current.
diveLevel: Open Water; Advanced for deeper sites.

quotes.text: Atlantic biodiversity meets new volcanic geology. The 2011 eruption site is alive and well.

goodToKnow.title: Getting there
goodToKnow.body: Fly Tenerife + ferry to El Hierro (3h).

goodToKnow.title: Quiet island
goodToKnow.body: El Hierro has fewer than 11,000 residents. Slow pace.

extendedDescription: Vis is the most remote inhabited Croatian island and the country's best diving — Adriatic walls, the B-17 bomber wreck and several other WWII sites, and Mediterranean fish life on clear water.
seasonNotes: May–October; July–August is warmest. Winter is too cold for most.
tripDuration: 5–7 nights as part of an Adriatic trip.
diveStyle: Boat diving on walls and wrecks; mild current.
diveLevel: Open Water; Advanced for the deeper wrecks (40m+).

quotes.text: A B-17 sitting on a sand bottom at 70m, intact. Vis is for wreck divers willing to come this far.

goodToKnow.title: Getting there
goodToKnow.body: Fly Split, then ferry to Vis (2h).

goodToKnow.title: Deep wrecks
goodToKnow.body: Several wrecks sit at 50–70m+. Tech training opens them up.

extendedDescription: Gozo (Malta's smaller sister island) is the Mediterranean's best shore-diving — limestone caves, blue holes, dramatic arches (the now-collapsed Azure Window's underwater remains), and the Inland Sea swim-through.
seasonNotes: May–October; July–September is warmest (24–27°C). Winter dips to 15°C.
tripDuration: 5–7 nights with a rental car for shore diving.
diveStyle: Shore diving on limestone topography; some swim-throughs and caverns.
diveLevel: Open Water; Advanced for the deeper caves and tunnels.

quotes.text: Pull up to a roadside parking, walk down stone steps, drop into a 25m blue hole. Gozo does Mediterranean shore diving better than anywhere.

goodToKnow.title: Getting there
goodToKnow.body: Fly Malta (MLA), ferry to Gozo (25 min).

goodToKnow.title: Rental car essential
goodToKnow.body: Most sites are roadside; a car makes the whole island accessible.

extendedDescription: The Azores' Santa Maria and surrounding islands sit mid-Atlantic — blue water diving on seamounts with mobula rays, devil rays, sharks (mako, blue), and exceptional water clarity. Big-animal Atlantic diving without the tropics.
seasonNotes: June–October; July–September is best for mobulas and pelagics.
tripDuration: 5–7 nights.
diveStyle: Blue-water diving at offshore seamounts; some current.
diveLevel: Advanced + pelagic comfort.

quotes.text: Hundreds of mobula rays in formation at Princess Alice Bank. The Azores is Portugal's hidden big-animal site.

goodToKnow.title: Getting there
goodToKnow.body: Fly Lisbon → Ponta Delgada or Santa Maria.

goodToKnow.title: Long boat rides
goodToKnow.body: Some seamounts are 2h offshore; pre-medicate.

extendedDescription: Capo Carbonara off Sardinia's southeast coast is a marine protected area — granite and limestone walls, posidonia seagrass meadows, octopus, grouper, and Roman shipwrecks scattered on the bottom.
seasonNotes: May–October; July–August is warmest.
tripDuration: 3–5 nights as part of a Sardinia trip.
diveStyle: Boat diving on walls and historic wrecks; mild current.
diveLevel: Open Water; Advanced for the deeper wrecks.

quotes.text: Romans sank ships here 2,000 years ago and the amphorae are still on the bottom. Where else.

goodToKnow.title: Getting there
goodToKnow.body: Fly Cagliari (CAG), 1h drive to Villasimius.

goodToKnow.title: Archaeological rules
goodToKnow.body: Strict no-touch on wrecks; criminal penalties for removal.

extendedDescription: Kornati National Park is a Croatian archipelago of 89 islands — rocky walls, posidonia meadows, and a protected marine ecosystem. Atmospheric Mediterranean diving with the bonus of Croatia's tourism infrastructure.
seasonNotes: May–October; July–August is warmest. Visibility best in spring and autumn.
tripDuration: 3–5 nights, often combined with sailing.
diveStyle: Boat diving on walls; mild current.
diveLevel: Open Water; Advanced for the deeper walls.

quotes.text: Sailing the Kornati with a tank rack on the deck — the Mediterranean as it should be done.

goodToKnow.title: Getting there
goodToKnow.body: Fly Zadar (ZAD) or Split (SPU), boat from Murter or Šibenik.

goodToKnow.title: Park fee
goodToKnow.body: Modest entry fee per day in the marine park.

extendedDescription: The Florida Keys offer the US's most accessible reef and wreck diving — the Christ of the Abyss statue, the USS Spiegel Grove and USS Vandenberg artificial reef wrecks, plus living coral reef tract from Key Largo to Key West.
seasonNotes: Year-round; May–September is warmest. Hurricane risk June–November.
tripDuration: 3–7 nights with rental car along the Keys.
diveStyle: Boat diving on reef and wreck; mild-to-moderate current.
diveLevel: Open Water for most reef; Advanced for the deeper wrecks (30m).

quotes.text: The Vandenberg is one of the great Americas wreck dives. Two days isn't enough to see all of it.

goodToKnow.title: Getting there
goodToKnow.body: Fly Miami (MIA) or Key West (EYW); drive the Overseas Highway.

goodToKnow.title: Coral status
goodToKnow.body: Reefs have been hit hard by bleaching and disease; protected sites are recovering.

extendedDescription: Kona on the Big Island of Hawai'i is one of the world's best manta dives — the Kona manta night dive, where mantas feed on plankton attracted by divers' lights — plus pelagic blackwater diving and reef sites along the leeward coast.
seasonNotes: Year-round; April–October is calmest. Mantas are year-round but most reliable in summer.
tripDuration: 5–7 nights.
diveStyle: Reef diving by day, night manta dive at a sand bowl, optional blackwater diving offshore.
diveLevel: Open Water for manta dive (snorkel option too); Advanced for blackwater.

quotes.text: A manta with a 4m wingspan barrel-rolling six inches from my mask in a halo of light — the Kona night dive is on the bucket list for a reason.

goodToKnow.title: Getting there
goodToKnow.body: Fly Kona (KOA) direct from US West Coast.

goodToKnow.title: Blackwater
goodToKnow.body: Offshore drift in 3,000m of water at night, looking at planktonic critters. Once-in-a-life experience.

extendedDescription: California's Channel Islands National Park offers kelp-forest diving — bull kelp cathedrals, garibaldi (the state fish), giant black sea bass, sea lions, and the occasional white shark passing through. Cold-water diving with personality.
seasonNotes: Year-round; September–November is warmest and clearest (15–17°C). Winter dips to 12°C.
tripDuration: Day-trips or 2–3 night liveaboard from Santa Barbara or Ventura.
diveStyle: Boat diving in kelp; mild current; cold water.
diveLevel: Open Water + cold-water comfort; drysuit recommended.

quotes.text: Floating through a kelp cathedral with sea lions zipping past — California gives you something the tropics can't.

goodToKnow.title: Getting there
goodToKnow.body: Drive to Santa Barbara or Ventura, day-boats from there.

goodToKnow.title: Water temp
goodToKnow.body: 7mm + hooded vest minimum; drysuit common.

extendedDescription: Jardines de la Reina ('Gardens of the Queen') is a 250km reef system off Cuba's southern coast — strictly protected, accessible only by liveaboard, with the densest shark and grouper population in the Caribbean. Often called the healthiest Caribbean reef.
seasonNotes: November–June. Closed in summer hurricane season.
tripDuration: 7-night liveaboard from Júcaro.
diveStyle: Wall and reef diving with mild-to-moderate current; baited shark dives.
diveLevel: Advanced recommended.

quotes.text: Caribbean reefs as they looked in 1950. Cuba's diving secret has been the embargo.

goodToKnow.title: Getting there
goodToKnow.body: Fly Havana, transfer to Júcaro (6h).

goodToKnow.title: US travelers
goodToKnow.body: Check current US travel regulations to Cuba; rules have shifted multiple times.

extendedDescription: Silver Bank is a shallow Atlantic seamount where 5,000+ humpback whales gather each winter to mate and calve — one of the world's two licensed in-water humpback swim destinations (the other is Tonga). Snorkel/freedive only; no scuba.
seasonNotes: January–April only.
tripDuration: 7-night liveaboard from Puerto Plata.
diveStyle: Snorkel only — scuba is not permitted at Silver Bank.
diveLevel: Comfortable swimmer; freediving experience helpful but not required.

quotes.text: Eye contact with a humpback calf and its mother holding station 5m below — I'll never see anything more powerful.

goodToKnow.title: Getting there
goodToKnow.body: Fly Puerto Plata (POP).

goodToKnow.title: Swim ethics
goodToKnow.body: Strict in-water rules: 30m approach distance, no chasing, guide-led only.

extendedDescription: Anse Chastanet on Saint Lucia's southwest coast offers easy shore diving directly off a black-sand beach beneath the Pitons — coral-rubble slopes, seahorses, frogfish, and reef fish in clear Caribbean water.
seasonNotes: Year-round; December–May is driest. Hurricane risk August–October.
tripDuration: 5–7 nights resort-based.
diveStyle: Shore and short-boat diving on volcanic slopes; mild current.
diveLevel: Open Water.

quotes.text: Wade off the beach beneath the Pitons and find a seahorse in five minutes. Saint Lucia diving is underrated.

goodToKnow.title: Getting there
goodToKnow.body: Fly Saint Lucia (UVF), 1.5h drive to Soufrière.

goodToKnow.title: Marine reserve
goodToKnow.body: Anse Chastanet sits in a marine protected area; ops handle daily fees.

extendedDescription: Speyside on Tobago's northeast coast is the country's diving capital — strong Atlantic currents, healthy soft coral, the world's largest brain coral (10m+ across), and reliable manta encounters. The diving is current-driven and not beginner-friendly.
seasonNotes: Year-round; January–May has calmest seas and best viz. Mantas February–June.
tripDuration: 5–7 nights at one of a few small lodges.
diveStyle: Drift diving in strong Atlantic current; negative entries.
diveLevel: Advanced + current comfort.

quotes.text: Strongest current I've drifted in the Caribbean. Mantas hover into it; we just blew past.

goodToKnow.title: Getting there
goodToKnow.body: Fly Tobago (TAB), 1.5h drive to Speyside.

goodToKnow.title: Atlantic side
goodToKnow.body: Conditions can be rough; trips cancel for swell.

extendedDescription: Larnaca is Cyprus's main dive hub — the Zenobia, a 170m ferry that sank in 1980, sits upright at 18–42m and is consistently ranked among Europe's top ten wreck dives. The rest of the coastline offers reef and cave diving in calm, warm Mediterranean water.
seasonNotes: May–October is dive season. July–August is warmest (27°C) but busiest. Spring and autumn offer calmer water and fewer crowds.
tripDuration: 3–5 days; easily combined with Paphos or Nicosia.
diveStyle: Wreck penetration and reef diving; easy conditions, mild current.
diveLevel: Open Water for the reef; Advanced or guided for full Zenobia penetration.

quotes.text: The Zenobia gets ranked with Truk and the SS Thistlegorm — that's not hype. It's genuinely that good for a wreck.

goodToKnow.title: Getting there
goodToKnow.body: Fly to Larnaca International (LCA) — well-served from across Europe. Dive centres are 5–10 minutes from the airport and will meet you there.

goodToKnow.title: Zenobia tip
goodToKnow.body: Book the wreck early — morning slots before other operators arrive have the best viz inside the truck deck.

extendedDescription: Salt Island's main draw is the RMS Rhone, a 310-ft Royal Mail Ship that went down in an 1867 hurricane in two pieces — bow at 24m, stern at 6m. The wreck is home to thousands of fish, soft coral overgrowth and the occasional spotted drum. The shallow stern section is accessible to Open Water divers.
seasonNotes: December–May is the dry season with best conditions. June–November is hurricane season — offshore weather can close the site on short notice.
tripDuration: 1–2 days from Tortola; typically a day-trip from Road Town.
diveStyle: Wreck diving; the stern is shallow and friendly, the bow rewards more advanced divers.
diveLevel: Open Water for the stern; Advanced for the bow section.

quotes.text: The Rhone is what got me into wreck diving. Once you see a ship that big sitting on the seafloor it rewires something.

goodToKnow.title: Getting there
goodToKnow.body: Fly to Terrence B. Lettsome International (EIS) on Beef Island, or via San Juan (SJU). Day-boat operators run from Road Town, Tortola — Salt Island is a 30–40 minute boat ride.

goodToKnow.title: Park permit
goodToKnow.body: BVI National Parks Trust day permit required. Most operators include it.

extendedDescription: Osprey Reef is a remote Coral Sea atoll 350km from Cairns with no roads, no hotels and no day-trippers — only liveaboards. The walls drop 1,000m in every direction and the North Horn shark feed has been running for decades. Silvertips and grey reef sharks come in close.
seasonNotes: August–November is the classic season when the trades ease. August–September has the best visibility. The atoll is reachable year-round but July and October are the most reliable weather windows.
tripDuration: 7–10 day liveaboard from Cairns; often combined with Ribbon Reefs.
diveStyle: Wall diving and open-ocean blue-water; strong current possible.
diveLevel: Advanced; 50+ dives recommended for the shark feed and wall dives.

quotes.text: Nothing in the Great Barrier Reef proper prepares you for how clear and blue the water is out here.

goodToKnow.title: Getting there
goodToKnow.body: Fly to Cairns (CNS). Liveaboards depart Cairns Marina — operators pick up from Cairns accommodation. The crossing is roughly 15–18 hours overnight.

goodToKnow.title: Seasickness
goodToKnow.body: The Coral Sea crossing can be rough. Pre-medicate the night before departure.

extendedDescription: Malmok beach on Aruba's leeward northwest shore sits above the Antilla, a 400-ft German freighter scuttled in 1940 — the Caribbean's largest accessible wreck. The site's sheltered from Aruba's near-constant trade wind, giving year-round diveable conditions in warm, clear water.
seasonNotes: Year-round. Aruba is outside the hurricane belt and gets consistent 27–29°C water. May–August brings the calmest seas.
tripDuration: 4–6 nights; Aruba is a full island destination with plenty to do above water.
diveStyle: Wreck and reef diving; calm conditions, easy entry.
diveLevel: Open Water; the Antilla's upper structure is accessible at 18m.

quotes.text: The Antilla is the rare Caribbean wreck where you can genuinely spend a whole week diving the same site and still find new rooms.

goodToKnow.title: Getting there
goodToKnow.body: Fly to Queen Beatrix International (AUA) — direct flights from most US hubs and Europe. Malmok is a 15-minute drive north from the airport along the leeward coast.

goodToKnow.title: Truck rental
goodToKnow.body: Most dive ops offer a gear-and-truck package. Worth it for the independence to hit the site before the crowds.

extendedDescription: Tela sits on Honduras's Caribbean north coast where an anomaly has captured global scientific attention: the reefs here are thriving. While coral cover across the wider Caribbean has collapsed by more than 50% since the 1980s, the reefs around Tela's Jeannette Kawas National Park have maintained densities of elkhorn and staghorn coral that marine biologists haven't seen elsewhere in the basin for decades. Scientists call it the 'Rebel Reef' — a reef that refuses to follow the script. The leading hypotheses involve cold-water upwellings from the continental shelf that buffer sea surface temperatures during bleaching events, nutrient inputs from protected wetland river systems, and unusually low local fishing pressure within the park boundary. Research teams from institutions across North America and Europe maintain permanent monitoring transects here, and the central question — whether Tela's resilience can be understood well enough to replicate protective conditions elsewhere in the Caribbean — has become one of coral science's most urgent problems.
seasonNotes: March–July is ideal: dry season, calm bay, 20–30m visibility. August–October brings rain and reduced viz from river runoff. November–February sees occasional cold fronts (nortes) with short choppy seas.
tripDuration: 4–7 nights.
diveStyle: Shallow-to-mid reef, boat diving from Tela town; calm protected bay with gentle current.
diveLevel: Open Water. Research Transect dives benefit from good buoyancy control to avoid coral contact.

quotes.text: I've dived the Caribbean for twenty years. I had genuinely forgotten what a healthy reef looked like until I came here.
The scientists in the water with us kept stopping to photograph coral. That told me everything.

goodToKnow.title: Getting there
goodToKnow.body: Fly San Pedro Sula (SAP) — 1.5h by road to Tela. Or fly Tegucigalpa (TGU) + 4h bus. No direct international flights to Tela itself.

goodToKnow.title: Park permit
goodToKnow.body: Diving inside Jeannette Kawas National Park (Punta Sal) requires a park entry fee, usually arranged by your dive operator.

goodToKnow.title: Research etiquette
goodToKnow.body: Do not touch or anchor near any tagged coral colonies or yellow-flagged monitoring stations. The data sets run back decades.

goodToKnow.title: Rainy season viz
goodToKnow.body: August–October river runoff can drop visibility to 8–12m near river mouths. Outer bay sites stay clearer.
